spots.name: Rynne Beach / Pawtucket Blvd / Lowell Riverwalk North
spots.note: Where the riverwalk meets the beach

spots.name: Wamesit Falls Overlook
spots.note: Dramatic falls on the Concord River

spots.name: The Millstream Falls
spots.note: Hidden gem at 105 Mill Rd. Park across the street, knock on the white door — if no answer, walk the property.

spots.name: Wachusett Mountain 360° Vista
spots.note: Full panoramic summit views — highest point nearby

spots.name: Old Stone Church View
spots.note: Ruins on the Wachusett Reservoir shore

spots.name: Walden Pond
spots.note: National Historic Landmark — where Thoreau lived 1845–47. Crystal clear glacial water, no streams in or out.

spots.name: Winter Island Beach
spots.note: Rocky shore with Fort Pickering Lighthouse (1871). Walk the breakwater for close views.

spots.name: Singing Beach
spots.note: Sand that squeaks when you walk — unique

spots.name: Blue Hills Observatory
spots.note: Weather observatory with Boston skyline views

spots.name: Dresser Hill View
spots.note: Elevated hilltop with far-reaching views — spectacular sunsets over rolling hills

spots.name: Cascading Waters
spots.note: 60-foot roadside waterfall in Worcester — powerful in spring. Park at 135 Olean St.

spots.name: Shining Rock Trail
spots.note: Former granite quarries with sweeping Blackstone River Valley views from rocky ledges.

spots.name: Tucker Brook Falls
spots.note: Wide 12-foot cascade fanning over rocky ledges — Tucker Brook Town Forest, Milford NH.

spots.name: Lower Purgatory Falls
spots.note: 12-foot waterfall splitting over a massive glacial boulder — gushes in spring.

spots.name: Mount Monadnock
spots.note: Iconic lone peak (3,165 ft) in Jaffrey NH — one of the most-climbed mountains in the world.

spots.name: Pack Monadnock
spots.note: 2,290 ft summit in Miller State Park, Peterborough NH — auto road to the top.

spots.name: Gleason Falls
spots.note: 10-foot cascade tumbling under a historic stone arch bridge, Hillsborough NH.

spots.name: Mount Watatic
spots.note: 1,832 ft summit with 360° views spanning Wachusett, Monadnock, and Boston skyline.

spots.name: Crane Beach
spots.note: 4-mile barrier beach with rolling dunes — Ipswich. Part of the Crane Estate.

spots.name: Castle Hill (Crane Estate)
spots.note: Grand Stuart-style mansion on a hilltop with Grand Allée sloping down to the Atlantic.

spots.name: Halibut Point State Park
spots.note: Abandoned granite quarry at Cape Ann's tip with rocky Atlantic shoreline views.

spots.name: Motif No. 1 / Bearskin Neck
spots.note: Famous red fishing shack called the most-painted building in America — Rockport harbor.

spots.name: Cochituate State Park
spots.note: 625-acre lake park in Natick — swimming, boating, and woodland trails.

spots.name: Frances Perkins Branch Library At Greendale Library
Cathedral Ledge
spots.note: 700-foot granite cliff above Echo Lake; drivable summit with sweeping Mount Washington Valley views. Popular with rock climbers.

spots.name: Crawford Notch View 2
spots.note: 1352 Crawford Notch Rd / Bartlett, NH, 03812, USA

spots.name: Mount Washington Hotel
spots.note: 308 Mt Washington Hotel Rd / Bretton Woods, NH, 03575, USA

spots.name: Quabbin Tower
spots.note: Quabbin Observation Tower on Quabbin Hill — panoramic views over the reservoir

spots.name: Rockport Wharf
spots.note: 99 T Whrf / Rockport Twn, MA, 01966, USA

spots.name: Alpine Adventures Outdoor Recreation
spots.note: There are 14 zip-lines on 2 separate courses covering over 2 miles of exhilaration through and above the treetops at heights of up to 200′ above the forest floor.

spots.name: Cannon Mountain Aerial Tramway
spots.note: An 80-passenger cable car brings visitors to the 4,080-foot summit of Cannon Mountain in under ten minutes. On a clear day, you can see the mountains of four states and Canada! At the summit, you’ll find spectacular scenic walking paths, a 360 degree observation deck, a cafeteria, bar and restrooms.

spots.name: Castle in the Clouds
spots.note: When we come here with the kids we spend most of the day on the property. The house itself has breathtaking views of Lake Winnipesaukee and the hills and mountains beyond. There are miles of hiking trails on the site as well. We usually eat a light lunch in the old horse barn, were the tables are set up in the stalls. You can also take a tour of Castle Springs bottling plant and see the actual spring that produces the water for bottling.

spots.name: Cathedral of the Pines
spots.note: Situated on a hilltop with a panoramic view of Grand Monadnock, the Cathedral of the Pines is a breathtaking open-air cathedral and meeting space on 236 acres. Our historic monuments honor the service of American men and women—both military and civilian.

spots.name: Conway Scenic Railroad
spots.note: Enjoy some of the finest natural scenery in the East as you travel through spectacular Crawford Notch – past sheer bluffs, steep ravines, cascading brooks and streams, panoramic mountain vistas, and across the famed Frankenstein Trestle and Willey Brook Bridge – enroute to Crawford Depot or Fabyan Station. Live commentary covers history and folklore of the railroad and area, as well as points of interest

spots.name: Flume Gorge, The
spots.note: An 800-foot gorge that extends from the base of Mount Liberty. Geological formation, Scenic/historic walking area, Waterfall

spots.name: Madison Boulder
spots.note: The Madison Boulder is one of the largest known glacial erratics in North America and among the largest in the world; it is preserved in the 17-acre Madison Boulder Natural Area in Madison, New Hampshire.

spots.name: Mt Washington Auto Road
spots.note: Opened in 1861, the Mt. Washington Auto Road is America’s Oldest Manmade Tourist Attraction. Experience the wonders of Mt. Washington’s weather, scenery and history.

spots.name: Mt Washington Cog Railway
spots.note: The Cog Railway is one of the world’s great railway adventures. The track runs up a three mile-long trestle with a maximum gradient of over 37%, making it the second-steepest mountain climbing train in the world and the only one entirely built on a trestle.

spots.name: Pemi Valley Excursions Moose Tour
spots.note: Join in the fun as we take a 2 1/2 to 3 hour journey through the majestic White Mountains in our air conditioned bus in search of the gentle giant of the North Country. Our nightly excursions feature a guided tour of the local area, a video about Moose, and interesting historical sites along the way.

spots.name: Redstone Rocket
spots.note: Only Town With a Real Redstone Missile. The same kind of engine booster used as a to hurl New Hampshire native Alan Shepard into space. Brought here in 1971.

spots.name: Rocky Gorge Scenic Area
spots.note: Foot bridge over a scenic rocky gorge. Mile long hike around a fishing pond. Restrooms available.

spots.name: Sabbaday Falls
spots.note: Pleasant 0.3 mile walk to a scenic waterfall, by the Kancamagus Highway. Restrooms available.

spots.name: Squam Lakes Natural Science Center
spots.note: Exhibits many different wild animals, including river otters, mountain lions, bobcats, deer, bears, birds, foxes, and skunks. Most are orphaned, injured, or otherwise unable to survive in the wild. Hiking and lake cruises also available.

spots.name: Wright Museum of WWII
spots.note: A World War II Museum located in Wolfeboro, featuring authentic displays of Home Front America between the years 1939 and 1945. The displays are truly amazing. They have Zero’s hanging from the ceiling, amfibs, tanks of several types, and lots of informative displays. Really a great place to stop when in the Lake Winnipesaukee area.

spots.name: Battleship Cove
spots.note: World’s largest historic naval ship exhibit, including USS Massachusetts.

spots.name: Bridge of Flowers
spots.note: 400 foot trolley bridge converted into a unique garden. Worth a stop during a Mohawk Trail Ride.

spots.name: Bunker Hill Monument
spots.note: The monument was erected to commemorate the Battle of Bunker Hill, the first major conflict between British and Patriot forces in the Revolutionary War, fought there June 17, 1775.

spots.name: Clara Barton Homestead
spots.note: The Clara Barton Birthplace Museum is open seasonally and by appointment. Guests enjoy a rich learning experience touring Clara’s childhood home.

spots.name: New Bedford Whaling Museum
spots.note: Museum in the former hub of 19th century whaling.

spots.name: Old North Bridge
spots.note: On April 19, 1775, the American Revolution began at Lexington and Concord with a clash of arms known to history as “the shot heard round the world.

spots.name: Snoopy Rock
spots.note: Large roadside rock painted to look like Snoopy.

Located on the second floor of the Simons
Public restroom
spots.note: Use Church St entrance, public bathroom is on first floor. Hours can change, currently 8am-430, 7 days. One of the few that stay open in winter.
